scope_note: QA Pisano Mod-24 Applied Cosmos Period cert [306]. Primary sources: Hardy, G.H. and Wright, E.M. (2008) An Introduction to the Theory of Numbers, Oxford, ISBN 978-0-19-921986-5, Ch.X (Fibonacci) and Ch.V (CRT); Wall, D.D. (1960) Fibonacci Primitive Roots, Amer. Math. Monthly 67(6):525-532 DOI 10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541 (Pisano periods). CLAIM: (C1) Every orbit in {1,...,24}^2 under QA T-step has period dividing pi(24)=24; maximum period=24; period spectrum={1,3,6,8,12,24}; histogram {1:1, 3:3, 6:12, 8:8, 12:48, 24:504}; all claims exhaustively verified. (C2) T(24,24)=(24,24): unique period-1 fixed point; characterized by 24|gcd(b,e); exact analog of mod-9 Singularity at (9,9). (C3) The 8 states {8,16,24}^2 \ {(24,24)} all have period exactly 8; characterized by 8|gcd(b,e) and 3 not| gcd(b,e); structural parallel: mod-9 Satellite = {3,6,9}^2 \ {(9,9)}, mod-24 Satellite = {8,16,24}^2 \ {(24,24)} (the m/3-multiples pattern). (C4) Applied Cosmos = 504 states with period 24 = 21 orbits x 24; exact characterization: 3 not| gcd(b,e) AND 8 not| gcd(b,e); count by inclusion-exclusion: 576 - 64 - 9 + 1 = 504 where 64=|{3|gcd}|, 9=|{8|gcd}|, 1=|{24|gcd}|. (C5) Closing the loop: max orbit period in {1,...,24}^2 = 24 = pi(24) = lcm(pi(3),pi(8)) = lcm(8,12) (cert [302]) = pi(9) = Cosmos period (cert [291]); the applied QA modulus 24 is period-self-consistent: its Pisano period equals the theoretical Cosmos cycle. Builds on cert [291] (pi(9)=24, Cosmos 72 states period-24), cert [298] (1+8+72 v3-stratification), cert [301] (3-adic tower), cert [302] (pi(8)=12, pi(24)=24 via CRT). Lineage: derived 2026-06-02.
